title: gtq-15-why-reality-needs-three

lossless_compression.reason: No live source file found for semantic addressing.

extracted_equations.text: = actualization (real outcome in time). Plain English: The most empirically confirmed equation in physics. It cannot be reduced to two operations or expanded to four without measurement breaking. Three is the minimum complete set. Three-Operation Constraint (Schematic)
chi_local = G * M * E * S_eff * T * K * R * Q * F * C
$\chi = \iiint(G\cdot M\cdot E\cdot S\cdot T\cdot K\cdot R\cdot Q\cdot F\cdot C)\, dx\, dy\, dt$. Framework's central coherence equation. (Defined in Article 08.)

translated_spans.original: $\chi = \iiint(G\cdot M\cdot E\cdot S\cdot T\cdot K\cdot R\cdot Q\cdot F\cdot C)\, dx\, dy\, dt$. Framework's central coherence equation. (Defined in Article 08.)
translated_spans.translated: Total coherence is the product of all ten factors, and if any one collapses to zero the output collapses with it.

claims.text: This article shows that any system capable of producing reality — not metaphorical reality, but actual physical existence — requires exactly three irreducible operations: possibility (the source of potential states), measurement (the selection of one state from many), and actualization (the instantiation of the selected state into physical existence).
